study_notes.title: Types of Skeletal Systems in Animals
study_notes.introduction: A skeleton is the supportive framework of bones or hard tissues in an organism. It provides shape, protection for delicate organs, and a surface for muscle attachment for movement. All animals are classified based on the type of skeleton they possess.

Hydrostatic_Skeleton.definition: A skeleton formed by a fluid-filled body cavity kept under pressure by surrounding muscles.
Hydrostatic_Skeleton.movement: Muscles squeeze against the internal fluid to change the body's shape and move.
examples: Slugs and Snails (the soft body parts)
Hydrostatic_Skeleton.vulnerability: These animals are soft and depend on moisture; if they lose their internal fluid or dry out, their body structure collapses.

Exoskeleton.definition: A hard, protective outer covering found on the outside of the animal's body.
Exoskeleton.composition: Mostly made of a tough, waterproof substance called Chitin.
Exoskeleton.limitation: The exoskeleton is rigid and does not grow. To increase in size, the animal must periodically shed its old shell in a process called Ecdysis (Moulting).

Endoskeleton.definition: An internal framework of hard tissues (bone and cartilage) found inside the flesh.
Endoskeleton.growth: Grows continuously with the animal; no shedding is required.
Endoskeleton.composition: Made of living bone tissue (hardened by Calcium and Phosphorus) and flexible cartilage.
examples: All Vertebrates (Mammals like Humans/Cows/Dogs, Birds, Fish, Reptiles, Amphibians)
Endoskeleton.unique_cases: Some animals like Tortoises have both an internal bony endoskeleton and a protective outer shell (exoskeleton).

functions_of_the_skeleton: Shape: Gives the body its specific framework.
Support: Keeps the body upright and prevents it from collapsing.
Protection: Shields delicate organs (e.g., Skull protects the Brain; Ribcage protects Heart and Lungs).
Movement: Provides surfaces for muscles to attach at joints, acting as levers.
Blood Manufacture: The Red Bone Marrow (in spongy bone) produces Red Blood Cells.
Storage: Acts as a bank for essential minerals like Calcium and Phosphorus.

key_biological_terms.Ecdysis_Moulting: The periodic shedding of an old exoskeleton to allow growth.
key_biological_terms.Ossification: The biological process where flexible cartilage turns into hard bone tissue.
key_biological_terms.Cartilage: A tough, flexible tissue found at bone ends to act as a cushion and shock absorber.

study_tips_for_ple: Remember: Insects shed their 'skin' (moulting) because their armor doesn't stretch.
Vertebrates have their 'bones on the inside', making them much better at supporting large body sizes.
Bone marrow isn't just one thing—Red marrow makes blood, while Yellow marrow stores fat.
